In a certain code language, ‘LILY’ is coded as ‘67’ and ‘LOTUS’ is coded as ’138’. How will ‘TULIP’ be coded in that language?
questions.explanation: LILY: L = 12, I = 9 (opposite 18), L = 12, Y = 25 12+18+12+25= 67 67×1 = 67 (1 is the number of vowels) LOTUS: L = 12, O = 15 (opposite 12), T = 20, U = 21 (opposite 6), S = 19 12+12+20+6+19 = 69 69×2=138 (2 is the number of vowels) Apply the Pattern to "TULIP" T = 20, U = 21 (opposite 6), L = 12, I = 9 (opposite 18), P = 16 20+6+12+18+16=72 72×2=144 (2 is the number of vowels)

questions.question: ‘A + B’ means ‘A is the mother of B’ ‘A - B’ means ‘A is the husband of B’ ‘A × B’ means ‘A is the father of B’ ‘A ÷ B’ means ‘A is the daughter of B’ Hence, if we consider S + Z – R ÷ H – P + U × M, then which of the following is NOT correct? > ‘A + B’ का अर्थ है ‘A, B की माँ है’ ‘A - B’ का अर्थ है ‘A, B का पति है’ ‘A × B’ का अर्थ है ‘A, B का पिता है’ ‘A ÷ B’ का अर्थ है ‘A, B की बेटी है’ अतः, यदि हम S + Z – R ÷ H – P + U × M पर विचार करें, तो निम्न में से कौन सा सही नहीं है?
options: P is the mother of R.>P, R की माँ है।
H is the paternal grandfather of M.>H, M का दादा है।
S is the mother – in - law of R. >S, R की सास है।
U is the son of S>U, S का पुत्र है।
questions.explanation: Clearly, U is not son of S. > स्पष्टतः, U, S का पुत्र नहीं है।

questions.question: Two positions of the same dice are shown. Which letter will be on the face opposite to the face with the letter F? > एक ही पासे की दो स्थितियाँ दर्शाई गई हैं। F अक्षर वाले फलक के विपरीत फलक पर कौन सा अक्षर होगा?
questions.explanation: B can be seen on both the dices with other 4 letters i.e. A, C, D and E. Only F is hidden, which must be opposite to B. > B को दोनों पासों पर अन्य 4 अक्षरों अर्थात A, C, D और E के साथ देखा जा सकता है। केवल F छिपा हुआ है, जो कि B के विपरीत होना चाहिए।

questions.question: Select the correct mirror image of the given figure when the mirror is placed at PQ as shown below. > दी गई आकृति की सही दर्पण छवि का चयन कीजिए जब दर्पण को PQ पर रखा गया है जैसा कि नीचे दिखाया गया है।
If 19 July 2000 was a Wednesday, then what would be the day of the week on 15 June 2012?
questions.explanation: We will count the total number of days from 19 July 2000 to 15 June 2012 and calculate the number of leap years and normal years. Years Breakdown • From 19 July 2000 to 19 July 2011 → 11 years • From 19 July 2011 to 15 June 2012 → Nearly 1 year Leap Years [Extra Day in February] Leap years: 2004, 2008, 2012 → 3 leap years Normal years: 2001, 2002, 2003, 2005, 2006, 2007, 2009, 2010, 2011 → 9 normal years Count Days from 19 July 2011 to 15 June 2012 • From 19 July 2011 to 19 June 2012 = 11 months • Subtract 4 days [from 19 June to 15 June] Days in each month • July 19 to December 31 → 166 days • January 2012 → 31 days • February 2012 [leap year] → 29 days • March 2012 → 31 days • April 2012 → 30 days • May 2012 → 31 days • 1 June to 15 June 2012 → 15 days Total days from 19 July 2011 to 15 June 2012: 166+31+29+31+30+31+15=333 days166 + 31 + 29 + 31 + 30 + 31 + 15 = 333 days 333÷7=47 full weeks+4 extra days Since 19 July 2011 was a Thursday, • +4 days → Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday → Friday Thus, 15 June 2012 was a Friday.

questions.question: In a class of 75 students, 40 students participate in Cricket, 28 students participate in Hockey, and 12 students participate in both Cricket and Hockey, whereas 19 students do not participate in any of the two sports. How many students participate only in Hockey?
questions.explanation: In a class of 75 students. 19 students do not participate in any of the two sports. Students who participate in any of the two sports or both the sports = 75 - 19 = 56 12 students participate in both Cricket and Hockey. 40 students participate in Cricket. Students who participate only in Cricket = 40 - 12 = 28 28 students participate in Hockey Students who participate only in Hockey = 28 - 12 = 16 Clearly, 16 students participate only in Hockey. Hence, '16' is the correct answer. Opt C.

questions.question: Which battle has led to the foundation of Mughal Empire?
questions.explanation: The First Battle of Panipat in 1526, where Babur defeated Ibrahim Lodi, the Sultan of Delhi, marked the beginning of the Mughal Empire in India. The battle demonstrated the power of gunpowder weapons, including cannons and muskets, which were effectively used by Babur's forces. The Battle of Chandawar, fought in 1194, saw Muhammad Ghori defeat Jayachandra. The Second Battle of Panipat was fought on November 5, 1556, between the Mughal army and the Hindu army of Hemu. The Battle of Chanderi, fought in 1528, pitted the Mughal forces of Babur against the Rajput forces led by Medini Rai.

questions.question: Who is considered as father of Indian Archaeology?
questions.explanation: Sir Alexander Cunningham, the first Director-General of the Archaeological Survey of India is considered the "father of Indian archaeology". He conducted the first excavations at Harappa in 1856 and 1872-73, making it the first site to be recognized on the world archaeological map. In 1905 partition of Bengal by Lord Curzon, divided it into East Bengal and West Bengal. Lord William Bentinck was the first Governor-General of British India. Warren Hastings was the first Governor-General of Bengal.

questions.question: Who among the following was the Viceroy of India during the formation of the Indian National Congress?
questions.explanation: Lord Dufferin was the Viceroy of India during the formation of the Indian National Congress. During Dufferin's tenure, the British annexed Upper Burma, ending its independence. The first census in India was conducted in 1872, during the tenure of Lord Mayo. Thomas Babington Macaulay, is famous for his role in shaping India's education policy. Lord Curzon is known for the partition of Bengal in 1905.

questions.question: Which folk dance is associated with Jammu and Kashmir?
questions.explanation: Folk dance is vital for preserving cultural traditions, celebrating community, and connecting people to their heritage. Rouf folk dance is associated with Jammu Kashmir. The Jhora dance is a folk dance primarily associated with the state of Uttarakhand. The Matki dance is a traditional folk dance from the Malwa region of Madhya Pradesh. Garba is a folk dance from the state of Gujarat.

questions.question: Which Bharat Ratna awardee was famous as a Karnatic classical vocalist?
questions.explanation: Bharat Ratna, India's highest civilian award was established on January 2, 1954 by India's first President Dr. Rajendra Prasad. The winners of this award have made remarkable contributions in their respective fields. Every year the Prime Minister nominates three people for this award. Karnatak Singer M. S. Subbulakshmi for Bharat Ratna award in 1998. Pandit Shivkumar Sharma was an Indian classical musician and santoor player. Pt Bhimsen Joshi is known for the khayal form of singing. Pandit Uday Shankar is renowned as the "Father of Modern Dance in India".

questions.question: According to Census 2011, which of the following States had negative population growth?
questions.explanation: The census is important to estimate the death rate and growth rate of the population of a country. According to the 2011 Census, Nagaland was the only Indian state to record a negative population growth rate. The decadal growth rate for Nagaland was -0.58%. In 2011, Kerala had the highest literacy rate in India, at 94%. In the 2011 census, Bihar had the lowest literacy rate in India, with an overall literacy rate of 61.80%. According to the 2011 census, Kerala had the highest sex ratio in India, with 1084 females per 1000 males, The lowest sex ratio in India, according to the 2011 Census, was observed in Haryana, with 877 females per 1000 males.

questions.question: Who is the author of ‘Adhe Adhure’?
questions.explanation: Mohan Rakesh's major plays include "Ashadh Ka Ek Din", "Lahron Ke Rajhans", and "Aadhe-Adhure". Mohan Rakesh has depicted social reality, human relationships, and the inconsistencies of modern society in his plays. Sumitranandan Pant, known for his nature-inspired poetry, wrote numerous poems, including "Gram Shree," "Chidambara," "Veena," "Pallav,". Stories of Premchand- Poos Ki Raat, Kafan, Bade Ghar Ki Beti,Boodhi Kaaki etc. Kamayani is a Hindi epic poem by Jaishankar Prasad.

questions.question: Sahara desert found in which Continent?
questions.explanation: Deserts are arid regions characterized by extremely low rainfall, receiving less than 10 inches [25cm] annually. There are various types of deserts, including hot and dry, semi-arid, coastal, and cold deserts. The Sahara Desert is the world's largest hot desert and the third largest desert behind Antarctica and the Arctic. Located in North Africa, it covers large sections of the continent.

questions.question: The first Common wealth Games were held in 1930 at-
questions.explanation: The Commonwealth is a voluntary association of 56 countries which work together towards shared goals in democracy and development. Each year on 24th May, Commonwealth Day is commemorated. Commonwealth Day is observed on the birthday of Queen Victoria of England. The 1st Commonwealth Games were held in 1930 at Hamilton, Canada. After Olympics, Commonwealth Games is the second-largest sports festival in the world. These Games are held once in four years.

questions.question: Which among the following Articles of the Indian Constitution provides for the Attorney General of India?
questions.explanation: The Attorney General of India is the highest law officer of the country, who serves as the chief legal advisor to the Central Government. Appointed by the President. Article 76 of the Indian Constitution deals with the Attorney General of India. Article 79 of the Constitution of India, concerning the "Constitution of Parliament". Article 72 of the Constitution of India gives the President the power to pardon and+ remit sentences. In Article 75 The Prime Minister shall be appointed by the President, and the other Ministers shall be appointed by the President on the advice of the Prime Minister.

questions.question: The Battle of Talikota was fought in the year?
questions.explanation: Vijayanagara Empire Founded in 1336, it was a powerful South Indian empire known for its cultural and economic prosperity. These were five Muslim-ruled states in the Deccan Plateau – Bijapur, Golconda, Ahmednagar, Berar and Bidar – which often clashed with one another. The Battle of Talikota was fought in 1565 between the Vijayanagara Empire and the allied forces of the Deccan Sultanates. The coalition of the Deccan Sultanates won, leading to the downfall of the Vijayanagara Emperor.

questions.question: Which pair of cities does the Yamuna Expressway connect?
questions.explanation: Yamuna Expressway connects Greater Noida-Agra. Yamuna Expressway or Taj Expressway is 165 km long with 6 lanes. It was inaugurated in 2012 and is India's longest motorway. The project was a Public-Private Partnership initiative developed by the Jaypee Group. The Ganga Expressway is a crucial infrastructure project in Uttar Pradesh, connecting Meerut to Prayagraj. It's a six-lane expressway spanning approximately 594 kilometers.

questions.question: 2024, which country’s men’s team won 11th Thomas Cup by defeating Indonesia?
questions.explanation: Uber Cup and Thomas Cup are associated with badminton. Thomas Cup is the world championship for men's national teams, while Uber Cup is for women's national teams. China achieved a historic feat, triumphing in both Thomas and Uber Cup finals against Indonesia. The men's team secured their 11th Thomas Cup, while the women's team claimed their 16th Uber Cup title with a flawless victory.

questions.question: Which one of the following Schedules of the Constitution of India contains provisions regarding anti-defection?
questions.explanation: The 10th Schedule of the Indian Constitution referred to as the 'Anti-Defection Law' was inserted by the 52nd Amendment [1985] to the Constitution. The Fifth Schedule of the Indian Constitution deals with the administration and control of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes in any state except Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, and Mizoram. The 2nd Schedule of the Indian Constitution outlines the emoluments, allowances, and privileges of various high-ranking officials, including the President of India, Governors of states, Speaker and Deputy Speaker of the Lok Sabha among others. The Third Schedule of the Indian Constitution outlines the forms of oaths or affirmations to be taken by various officials, including members of Parliament, Union Ministers, Supreme Court and High Court judges, and the Comptroller and Auditor General.

questions.question: Who will receive the Golden lion for life time Achievement award at the 2025 Venice film festival?
questions.explanation: The Golden Lion for Lifetime Achievement of the 82nd Venice International Film Festival of La Biennale di Venezia [August 27 – September 6, 2025] has been awarded to the great German director Werner Herzog. Martin Luther was a German theologian and religious reformer.

questions.question: Which layer of the Earth extends to a depth of about 2900 km?
questions.explanation: The Earth is composed of four main layers: the crust, the mantle, the outer core, and the inner core. The Earth's mantle layer extends to a depth of about 2900 km. It lies between the crust and the outer core. The upper part of the mantle is called the asthenosphere. The crust, along with the uppermost part of the mantle, makes up the lithosphere, which is divided into tectonic plates. The Earth's core is the extremely hot and dense center of the planet, made up of mostly iron and nickel.

questions.question: Which body is the regulator of the Money Market in the country?
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935 in accordance with the provisions of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934. The Reserve Bank of India [RBI] is the primary regulator of the money market in India. State Bank of India [SBI] is India's largest public sector bank, offering a wide range of financial services and products. UNDP works to alleviate poverty and reduce inequalities, providing support to countries in developing strategies to combat it. the Securities and Exchange Board of India, is a statutory body established to protect investor interests, regulate the securities market.

questions.question: Who has presented the cell theory?
questions.explanation: The cell theory is a foundational concept in biology, stating that all living organisms are composed of one or more cells, and that the cell is the basic unit of structure and function in living things. The cell theory was primarily developed by German scientists Schleiden and Schwann in 1838. The cell was first discovered and named by Robert Hooke in 1665. Robert Brown is famous for his discovery of the cell nucleus in 1831.

questions.question: What position has India got in the world Press Freedom Index 2024?
questions.explanation: In the 2024 World Press Freedom Index, India was ranked 159th out of 180 countries. India is currently ranked as the 5th largest economy globally in 2025. Climate Change Performance Index 2025, Indias rank is 10th.

questions.explanation: Tributary of Godavari is Indravati, tributary of Krishna is Tungabhadra, tributary of Mahanadi is Seonath and tributary of Yamuna is Chambal.

questions.question: When did the Viksit Bharat by 2047 scheme start?
questions.explanation: September 14, 2024 "Viksit Bharat 2047" is an initiative aimed at realizing Indias aspiration. Prime Minister’s vision is to actively involve the youth of the country in formulation of national plans, priorities and goals of the country. Economic growth, technological upgradation, infrastructure development, social empowerment, and sustainability are the criteria of this programme.

questions.question: What is the chemical formula of Alcohol?
questions.explanation: C2H5OH is the chemical formula of Alcohol. The general formula for the alcohols is CnH2n+1OH [where n is the number of carbon atoms in the molecule]. Borax is sodium tetraborate decahydrate [Na2B4O7 10H2O]. The chemical formula for glucose is C6H12O6. C2H5 represents the ethyl group.

questions.question: Newton/kg is a unit of:
questions.explanation: The rate of change of velocity with respect to time is called acceleration. Acceleration is a vector quantity because it has both magnitude and direction. The unit "newton per kilogram" [N/kg or N kg⁻¹] is the unit of acceleration. The SI unit of force is the Newton [N]. The SI unit of both work and Energy are the joule [J].

questions.question: Who is the present chairman of NITI Aayog?
questions.explanation: Planning Commission was replaced by a new institution – NITI Aayog on January 1, 2015. PM Narendra Modi is the present chairman of NITI Aayog. Shri B.V.R. Subrahmanyam is joined as Chief Executive Officer of NITI Aayog. Shri Suman Bery is currently Vice Chairperson, NITI Aayog.

questions.question: 5 years ago, the ratio the age of A to that of B was 4 : 5. Five years hence, the ratio of the age of A to that of B will be 6 : 7. If, at present, C is 10 years younger than B, then what will be the ratio of the present age of A to that of C?
questions.explanation: A's present age =25 B's present age =30 C = 30 – 10 = 20 = A : C = 5 : 4

The ratio in which these two mixtures should be mixed so as to make the ratio of the two liquids 7:3 is? > एक मिश्रण में दो द्रवों का अनुपात 3:5 है तथा दूसरे मिश्रण में 6:1 है। इन दोनों मिश्रणों को किस अनुपात में मिलाया जाना चाहिए कि दोनों द्रवों का अनुपात 7:3 हो जाए?

If the tickets for Screen 1, Screen 2, and Screen 3 are Rs 350, Rs 300, and Rs 250 respectively, then which screen has the maximum total revenue for three shows? > नीचे दिया गया बार चार्ट एक मल्टी-स्क्रीन थिएटर में 3 शो के लिए दर्शकों की संख्या दर्शाता है। यदि स्क्रीन 1, स्क्रीन 2 और स्क्रीन 3 के टिकट क्रमशः 350 रुपये, 300 रुपये और 250 रुपये हैं, तो किस स्क्रीन की तीन शो के लिए कुल आय अधिकतम है?

Three candidates A, B and C contested an election. Out of the total votes on a voter list 25% did not vote and 6.66% votes polled were invalid. C got 2450 valid votes, which were 40% more than that of B. If A got only 40% of the total votes, then find the number of votes by which A defeated C?
questions.explanation: Let, Voter list =100x No. of votes polled = 75x Valid votes =70x Given that, A got = 40x Hence, B and C together got 30x C got, 2450 which is 40% more than B, So, B got = 1750 30x = 4200 40x = 5600 (Votes polled for A) Votes by which A defeated C = 5600 - 2450 = 3150

questions.question: A dishonest trader marks up his goods by 80% and gives discount of 25%. Besides he gets 20% more amount per kg from wholesaler and sells 10% less per kg to customer. What is the overall profit percentage? > एक बेईमान व्यापारी अपने माल पर 80% अधिक मूल्य अंकित करता है और 25% की छूट देता है। इसके अलावा वह थोक विक्रेता से प्रति किलोग्राम 20% अधिक राशि प्राप्त करता है और ग्राहक को प्रति किलोग्राम 10% कम बेचता है। कुल लाभ प्रतिशत क्या है?
questions.explanation: → 100-180 → 4-3 →400---540 100:135 Effective selling price of 0.9kg is 135 He actually gets 1.2 kg of goods from the wholesaler Effective price of 1.2kg=100 Effective cost price of 0.9kg = [100/1.2]*0.9=75 75---------135 135-75/75 *100=80% > → 100-180 → 4-3 →400---540 100:135 0.9 किग्रा का प्रभावी विक्रय मूल्य 135 है वह थोक विक्रेता से वास्तव में 1.2 किग्रा माल प्राप्त करता है 1.2 किग्रा का प्रभावी मूल्य = 100 0.9 किग्रा का प्रभावी लागत मूल्य = [100/1.2]*0.9=75 75---------135 135-75/75 *100=80%

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
options: She is owning a new car
for the first time in her life
and she is very excited about it.
questions.explanation: The error lies in segment: She is owning a new car. Explanation: • The verb "owning" is incorrect in this context. • "Own" is a stative verb that describes possession and is not used in the progressive [-ing] form. • Since the sentence describes a state, the simple present tense "owns" should be used instead. Corrected Sentence: "She owns a new car for the first time in her life and she is very excited about it."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Orders have been falling off _______, we must advertise more.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Lately Explanation: The word "lately" fits best in this context as it refers to a recent time period, indicating that orders have been falling off in the recent past, prompting the need for advertising. Here are the meanings and examples of all options: • Eventually – अंततः, बाद में – Refers to something happening after a long period of time or as a final result. Example: After much effort, they eventually completed the project. • Initially – प्रारंभ में, शुरुआत में – Refers to the beginning or start of something. Example: The company initially faced many challenges before becoming successful. • Lately – हाल ही में, हाल के दिनों में – Refers to something that has happened recently or in the recent past. Example: We have been seeing fewer customers lately at the store. • Currently – वर्तमान में, अभी – Refers to something happening right now or at the present time. Example: I am currently working on an important project at the office. Final Correct Sentence: "Orders have been falling off lately, we must advertise more."

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. Little she knew that her words would inspire millions.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Little did she know Explanation: • The original sentence is grammatically incorrect. It does not follow the correct structure for an emphatic or negative adverbial phrase at the beginning. • When a sentence starts with "Little" [a negative adverb], we use inversion, i.e., we place the auxiliary verb before the subject. • So, instead of "Little she knew," the correct structure is "Little did she know." Examples: • Little he expected the truth. [Wrong] Little did he expect the truth. [right] • Never I saw such a thing. [Wrong] Never did I see such a thing. [right] Final Correct Sentence: "Little did she know that her words would inspire millions."

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: She studies hard lest she may fail.
She studies hard lest she should fail.
She studies hard lest she must fail.
She studies hard lest she will fail.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: She studies hard lest she should fail. Explanation: • The sentence is grammatically correct and follows the rule for using the correlative conjunction “lest – should.” • "Lest" means “for fear that” and is followed by a subject + should + base verb. • We do not use modals like may, will, must after lest — only "should" is correct. • Options [A], [C], and [D] are incorrect because "may", "must", and "will" are incorrect pairings with "lest". Concept Applied: • Correlative Conjunction Pair: Lest – should • Meaning: “She studies hard for fear that she might fail.” Final Correct Sentence: "She studies hard lest she should fail."

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. I really take back my mother in both looks and behaviour.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Take after Explanation: • The phrasal verb "take after" means to resemble a parent or older relative in appearance or behaviour, which fits the sentence context perfectly. • The use of "take back" is incorrect here, as it means to retract a statement or to return something, which doesn’t apply to the idea of resemblance. Meanings and examples of the options: • Take after – किसी की तरह होना – To resemble someone, usually a family member Example: He takes after his grandfather in temperament. • Take over – कार्यभार संभालना – To assume control or responsibility Example: She will take over the project from next week. • Take to – पसंद करना शुरू करना – To develop a liking for someone or something quickly Example: The dog took to the new trainer immediately. • Take back – वापस लेना – To withdraw a statement or return an item Example: I take back what I said in anger. Final Correct Sentence: "I really take after my mother in both looks and behaviour."

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. He is really keen ____ playing the guitar in his free time.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: On Explanation: • The adjective "keen" is correctly followed by the preposition "on" when expressing a strong interest or enthusiasm for something. • Other prepositions like "to", "of", or "with" do not correctly follow "keen" in this usage. Examples: She’s keen on joining the drama club. Keen to — Correct, but used with verbs, not nouns Usage: Shows a strong desire or eagerness to do something Structure: Keen to + base verb Examples: • He is keen to start his new job. • I’m keen to learn Spanish. Tip: Use "keen on" before activities [e.g., keen on singing] and "keen to" before actions [e.g., keen to perform]. Corrected Sentence: "He is really keen on playing the guitar in his free time."

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: to the hospital Explanation: • The article "the" is used incorrectly in this context. • When places like "hospital" are used for their primary purpose [e.g., treatment or medical care], no article is used. • We use "the" only when referring to the place as a specific location or building, not for its main function. Examples: • He was taken to hospital after the accident. ✔ • I visited the hospital to see my friend. ✔ [not for treatment] Corrected Sentence: The injured man was taken to hospital for a routine visit.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph: A. then it is very hard to change your life with any degree of consistency. B. If you aren’t aware of what you’re actually doing C. Trying to build better habits without self-awareness is like firing arrows into the night. D. You can’t expect to hit the bullseye if you’re not sure where the target is located.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: BACD Explanation: Let’s break it down step by step: • B introduces the condition: "If you aren’t aware of what you’re actually doing." • A completes it with the result: "then it is very hard to change your life with any degree of consistency." • C follows with a relevant analogy about habit-building and self-awareness. • D ends the paragraph with a strong visual metaphor reinforcing the point. Final Paragraph: If you aren’t aware of what you’re actually doing, then it is very hard to change your life with any degree of consistency. Trying to build better habits without self-awareness is like firing arrows into the night. You can’t expect to hit the bullseye if you’re not sure where the target is located.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. You should complete the project by tomorrow.
options: The project is completed by you by tomorrow.
The project should be completed by you by tomorrow.
The project should complete by tomorrow.
The project was to be completed by you by tomorrow.
questions.explanation: Explanation: • The original sentence contains the modal verb "should". • In passive voice, we use: modal + be + past participle [i.e., should + be + completed] • The agent "by you" is optional but acceptable here. Final Sentence: The project should be completed by you by tomorrow.

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. Devid said, "I cannot believe how fast time flies."
options: Devid said that he cannot believe how fast time flies.
Devid said that he could not believe how fast time flies.
Devid said that he could not believe how fast time flew.
Devid said he cannot believe how fast time flew.
questions.explanation: Explanation: • The original sentence is in direct speech and contains a statement: "I cannot believe how fast time flies." • In indirect speech, we follow the rules of tense back shifting: • "cannot" [present] changes to "could not" [past], • "flies" [simple present] changes to "flew" [simple past]. • This change is required because the reporting verb "said" is in the past tense, and the statement is not a universal truth—it refers to a personal expression of surprise. • Option A and Option B retain present tenses, which is incorrect. • Option D uses mixed tenses and omits “that,” making the sentence grammatically awkward. Structure used for reporting a personal feeling or statement: [Subject] + said + that + [past form of the sentence] Final Sentence: Devid said that he could not believe how fast time flew.

questions.question: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will visit nations in Africa and South America ____1____ the next few days and attend the upcoming BRICS Summit in Rio de Janeiro. Here is what to know - This will be Modi’s first bilateral visit to Ghana. John Mahama, who ____2____ President in January, visited India in 2015 for the India-Africa Forum Summit. Ghana is one of West Africa’s ____3____ growing economies; its relationship with India is marked by robust and expanding trade and investment. PM will ____4____ talks with President Mahama to review the bilateral partnership and discuss ways to ____5____ it through economic, energy, defence, and development cooperation. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 -
questions.explanation: Explanation – The phrase "over the next few days" is an idiomatic expression that means "during" or "in the course of", which fits perfectly in this context, implying that the visit will take place within the next few days. The other options don't fit as well: - Through: Typically implies movement or continuation from one side to the other, which doesn't fit this context. - Along: Implies movement or accompaniment, which doesn't fit this context. - Among: Implies being surrounded by or part of a group, which doesn't fit this context. So, the correct answer is "over".

questions.question: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will visit nations in Africa and South America ____1____ the next few days and attend the upcoming BRICS Summit in Rio de Janeiro. Here is what to know - This will be Modi’s first bilateral visit to Ghana. John Mahama, who ____2____ President in January, visited India in 2015 for the India-Africa Forum Summit. Ghana is one of West Africa’s ____3____ growing economies; its relationship with India is marked by robust and expanding trade and investment. PM will ____4____ talks with President Mahama to review the bilateral partnership and discuss ways to ____5____ it through economic, energy, defence, and development cooperation. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 -
questions.explanation: Explanation – The verb tense "was elected" indicates that John Mahama became President at a specific point in the past (January), which fits perfectly in this context. The other options don't fit as well: - Has elected: Implies a present perfect tense, which doesn't fit the context of visiting India in 2015. - Elected: Is a simple past tense without the correct grammatical structure (it would need "who became" or similar). - Had elected: Implies a past perfect tense, which would suggest that the election happened before another past event, but that's not the intended meaning here.

questions.question: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will visit nations in Africa and South America ____1____ the next few days and attend the upcoming BRICS Summit in Rio de Janeiro. Here is what to know - This will be Modi’s first bilateral visit to Ghana. John Mahama, who ____2____ President in January, visited India in 2015 for the India-Africa Forum Summit. Ghana is one of West Africa’s ____3____ growing economies; its relationship with India is marked by robust and expanding trade and investment. PM will ____4____ talks with President Mahama to review the bilateral partnership and discuss ways to ____5____ it through economic, energy, defence, and development cooperation. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3 -
questions.explanation: Explanation – The phrase "fastest growing" is a common idiomatic expression that describes economies or industries that are growing at a very rapid pace, which fits perfectly in this context. The other options don't fit as well: - Fast: Doesn't convey the same level of growth as "fastest growing". - Faster: Would be used for comparisons between two things, but here we're describing Ghana as one of the fastest growing economies in a group. - Fasten: Is a verb that means "to make something secure or firm", which doesn't fit the context.

questions.question: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will visit nations in Africa and South America ____1____ the next few days and attend the upcoming BRICS Summit in Rio de Janeiro. Here is what to know - This will be Modi’s first bilateral visit to Ghana. John Mahama, who ____2____ President in January, visited India in 2015 for the India-Africa Forum Summit. Ghana is one of West Africa’s ____3____ growing economies; its relationship with India is marked by robust and expanding trade and investment. PM will ____4____ talks with President Mahama to review the bilateral partnership and discuss ways to ____5____ it through economic, energy, defence, and development cooperation. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4 -
questions.explanation: Explanation – The verb "hold" means "to conduct or have a meeting or discussion", which fits perfectly in this context. The other options don't fit as well: - Holding: Is a present participle verb form that implies an ongoing action, which doesn't fit the future tense "will". - Held: Is a past tense verb form that doesn't fit the future tense "will". - Heading: Means "leading or directing", which doesn't fit the context of having talks.

questions.question: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will visit nations in Africa and South America ____1____ the next few days and attend the upcoming BRICS Summit in Rio de Janeiro. Here is what to know - This will be Modi’s first bilateral visit to Ghana. John Mahama, who ____2____ President in January, visited India in 2015 for the India-Africa Forum Summit. Ghana is one of West Africa’s ____3____ growing economies; its relationship with India is marked by robust and expanding trade and investment. PM will ____4____ talks with President Mahama to review the bilateral partnership and discuss ways to ____5____ it through economic, energy, defence, and development cooperation. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5 -
questions.explanation: Explanation –The verb "enhance" means "to improve or increase the quality or value of something", which fits perfectly in this context, implying that the partnership will be strengthened or improved. The other options don't fit as well: - Mortify: Means "to cause someone to feel embarrassed or humiliated", which is opposite of the intended meaning. - Abase: Means "to lower someone's pride or status", which is also opposite of the intended meaning. - Cheapen: Means "to reduce the value or quality of something", which is not what the sentence is trying to convey.

questions.question: Identify the word that is incorrectly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is – Connoisseur Let’s see the meaning of all words, Connoisseur-विशेषज्ञ-A person who has expert knowledge or appreciation of something. Renaissance-पुनर्जागरण-A period of cultural revival or rebirth, especially in art and learning. Schedule-अनुसूची -A plan that shows when things will happen or be done. Weird-अजीब-Strange or unusual.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence – The sunlight made the ocean water gleam brightly.
questions.explanation: The correct synonym is - Sheen Let’s see the meaning of all words, Gleam -चमक -A soft, shining light or glow Frisk-समीप जांच-To search someone by patting their body, typically to find concealed items Sheen-चमक-A soft glow or shine on a surface Grisly-भयानक-Causing horror or disgust, especially due to something gruesome Gratuitous-अवश्यक-Unnecessary or uncalled for, often in a negative context.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. EMACIATED
questions.explanation: The correct antonym is – Healthy Let’s see the meaning of all words, Emaciated-अत्यंतकमजोर-Extremely thin, especially due to malnutrition or illness Disparage-अपमानकरना-To speak about someone or something in a way that shows disapproval and lack of respect Exculpation-दोषमुक्ति-The act of clearing someone of blame or guilt Healthy-स्वस्थ-In good physical or mental condition; free from illness Angst-उदासी-A deep feeling of anxiety or dread, often about something uncertain or out of one's control.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. She was officially Nascent as a priest in the church ceremony last Sunday.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Ordained Let’s see the meaning of all words, Nascent-नवजात-Existing or coming into existence; just beginning to develop. Renouncement-त्याग-The act of giving up or rejecting something. Implicit-व्यक्त-अर्थहीन-Understood though not clearly or directly expressed. Populace-जनता-The people living in a particular country or area; the general public Ordained-आधिकारिक रूप से नियुक्त-Officially appointed to a role, often in a religious context.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. Be in the air
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Under consideration Let’s see the meaning of idiom, Be in the air-हवा में होना-Used to describe something uncertain, undecided, or unresolved.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. Obliterate
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Annihilate Let’s know the meaning of all words, Obliterate-नष्ट करना-To destroy completely, leaving no trace behind. Sarcastic-व्यंग्यात्मक-Using irony to mock or convey contempt. Dignified-आदर्शपूर्ण-Showing self-respect and honour in behaviour or appearance. Annihilate-नष्ट करना-To completely destroy or wipe out something or someone. Outraged-आक्रोशित-Feeling intense anger or shock due to something offensive or unjust.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- She spent an inordinate amount of time on her phone, ignoring her work completely.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - Reasonable Let’s see the meaning of all words, Inordinate-अत्यधिक-Excessive, more than what is considered reasonable. Exorbitant-अत्यधिक-Unreasonably high, especially in terms of price or cost. Reasonable-उचित-Being fair, sensible, or within acceptable limits. Optimistic-आशावादी-Having a positive outlook or expectation for the future. Considerate-सहनशील-Showing care and thoughtfulness towards others.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. One who has suddenly gained new wealth.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Parvenu Let’s see the meaning of all words, Parvenu-नया धनाड्य-A person who has recently acquired wealth or status, but is considered to lack the social qualities of those who have had it for a longer time. Silhouette-आकृति- A dark shape or outline of someone or something visible against a lighter background. Plenitude-पूर्णता-A state of abundance or completeness. Transcendent-परम-Going beyond ordinary limits; surpassing or exceeding.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Down in the dumps
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Sad and depressed Let’s see the meaning of idiom, Down in the dumps-उदास-Feeling sad, depressed, or in low spirits.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. Words that begin with the same letter, syllable or sound
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Alliteration Let’s see the meaning of all words, Metaphor-रूपक- A comparison between two things without using "like" or "as." Example: "Time is a thief." Simile-उपमेय- A comparison between two things using "like" or "as." Example: "Her smile is like the sun." Alteration-परिवर्तन- The act of changing or modifying something. Alliteration-अनुप्रास- The repetition of the same consonant sound at the beginning of words. Example: "She sells seashells by the seashore."
